acceptance.command: Review the README opening and M1 status section, then run the repository Quick and portable workflow-contract gates.
acceptance.proof: The README distinguishes accepted functional closure from unpublished branch state and keeps the cumulative accessibility matrix at Milestone 7.

commit_policy: Commit the reviewed unit transition first, then commit the README alignment with its passing Quick validation evidence. Do not mix implementation or publication changes.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Replace the stale active/pending wording with accepted-functional and publication-pending status.

non_scope: Changing Fleet, Manifold, Quest, LSL, WPF, Hub, CLI, API, media, relay, ADB, or File Manager behavior.
Claiming publication, release accessibility conformance, or a new device validation.
Rewriting the accepted Fleet M1 functional closeout or its evidence.
objective: Align the first-hop README status with the accepted Fleet M1 functional closeout while preserving the separate publication boundary and the deferred cumulative accessibility release gate.
outputs: A truthful first-hop M1 status with no runtime, device, feature, or publication claim.
